codeReview.summary: The commit cleanly updates README.md and wrangler.jsonc as specified. README.md now references Cloudflare Pages (not Vercel), includes a comprehensive Deploying section with CLI and Dashboard options, documents required env vars, and correctly notes the --legacy-peer-deps flag for npm install. wrangler.jsonc has comment blocks documenting GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN and GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET with usage context. The lock file is verified clean: no @vercel/analytics or @vercel/speed-insights, and @opennextjs/cloudflare is present. .gitignore includes .open-next. Build evidence from the handoff confirms exit code 0 with worker.js and assets/ generated, and 3 blog posts pre-rendered.

issues.description: Environment variable comments are placed after the closing brace of the JSON object. While valid JSONC (comments are allowed anywhere), placing them inside the object (e.g., before the closing brace) would be more conventional and clearly associated with the configuration. This is purely stylistic.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already correctly documents --legacy-peer-deps and env vars. README.md now mirrors this. No gaps found — the worker's changes are consistent with AGENTS.md conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md 'npm Install' section matches README.md 'Running Locally' section. AGENTS.md 'Environment Variables' section matches wrangler.jsonc comments and README.md env var docs.

summary: Pass. The commit (d1c0765) correctly updates README.md to reference Cloudflare Pages deployment with a full Deploying section and env var docs, and adds env var documentation comments to wrangler.jsonc. Lock file is clean (no Vercel packages, @opennextjs/cloudflare present). .gitignore includes .open-next. Build was verified successful (exit 0) with worker.js, assets/, and 3 pre-rendered blog posts. One minor stylistic note: wrangler.jsonc comments are placed outside the JSON object rather than inside, but this is non-blocking.
